description.en: Sri Lanka's flagship plantation crop, branded globally as Ceylon tea. Commercial cultivation began in 1867 at Loolecondera Estate, Kandy. Seven principal growing regions: Nuwara Eliya, Dimbula, Uva, Uda Pussellawa (high-grown), plus Kandy, Ruhuna, Sabaragamuwa (mid & low-grown). The Tea Research Institute (TRI) clonal series — TRI 2023 through TRI 2027 and later releases — covers over 55% of the national tea extent, replacing seedling plantations with vegetatively propagated high-yielding disease-resistant clones.

elevation_m.quote: altitudes from approximately 600 to 2,000 meters... broadly divided into low-grown (<600 m a.s.l), mid-grown (600–1200 m a.s.l) and high-grown (>1200 m a.s.l) areas

description.quote: clones such as TRI 2023 through TRI 2027, now cover over 55% of the nation's tea extent, enabling more uniform growth, disease resistance, and elevated made-tea yields compared to seedling plantations.
